title: Foundry Implementation: Text, Speech, Vision & Information Extraction
blueprintRef: Implement AI solutions using Microsoft Foundry (55-60%) > Text/Speech, Computer Vision/Image generation, Information extraction

questions.stem: Foundry offers two approaches to text analysis. What are they?

options.text: General-purpose AI models (prompt-driven) and Azure Language in Foundry Tools (deterministic analysers)

explanation.correct: Text analysis can use flexible general-purpose models via prompts, or purpose-built Azure Language analysers for deterministic structured output.

distractors.B: Those are speech workloads.
distractors.D: Those are deployment types, not text-analysis approaches.

questions.stem: To analyse text with a general-purpose model in a Python client, which API call is used?

explanation.correct: General-purpose text analysis uses the OpenAI Responses API via responses.create() with a natural-language prompt.

distractors.B: detect_language() is an Azure Language SDK method.

questions.stem: Which client class does the Azure Language SDK use for structured text analysis?

explanation.correct: The Azure Language SDK uses TextAnalyticsClient (with an AzureKeyCredential).

distractors.B: ContentUnderstandingClient is for information extraction.

questions.stem: Which Azure Language SDK method identifies the language of input text and returns an ISO code and confidence score?

explanation.correct: detect_language() returns the primary language, its ISO 639-1 code, and a confidence score.

distractors.B: recognize_pii_entities() finds personal data.
distractors.C: responses.create() is the general-purpose model call.

questions.stem: A workflow must find and redact personal data such as names and phone numbers in text. Which Azure Language SDK method is appropriate?

explanation.correct: recognize_pii_entities() detects PII and provides a redacted_text plus entity details with categories and confidence.

distractors.B: detect_language() identifies the language only.
distractors.D: Image generation is a vision capability.

questions.stem: A compliance pipeline needs identical, repeatable results for the same input every time. Which text-analysis approach should you choose?

explanation.correct: Azure Language gives deterministic, consistent structured output ideal for automated/compliance pipelines.

distractors.B: General-purpose models are probabilistic; identical calls may vary.
distractors.C: Image generation is unrelated to text.
distractors.D: Video generation is unrelated to text.

questions.stem: When is a general-purpose model preferable over Azure Language for text analysis?

options.text: For flexible exploration and compound tasks driven by natural-language prompts
When you need identical output on every call
When you must guarantee deterministic structured fields
When you only ever need PII redaction

explanation.correct: General-purpose models shine for flexible, prompt-driven, compound text tasks.

distractors.B: Determinism favours Azure Language.
distractors.C: Deterministic structured fields favour Azure Language.
distractors.D: Fixed single-task work favours the purpose-built analyser.

questions.stem: How can an agent combine its reasoning with deterministic Azure Language analysis without writing custom REST/auth code?

options.text: Connect the Azure Language MCP server as a tool

explanation.correct: The Azure Language MCP server exposes Language capabilities as tools an agent can discover and call without custom integration code.

distractors.B: Temperature is unrelated.

questions.stem: Which Python package provides the Azure Speech SDK for speech-to-text and text-to-speech?

explanation.correct: The Azure Speech SDK is provided by azure-cognitiveservices-speech.

distractors.B: That is the Azure Language SDK.
distractors.D: openai is the Responses API client.

questions.stem: Which combination of objects is used to perform speech-to-text with the Azure Speech SDK?

explanation.correct: STT uses SpeechConfig (auth/endpoint), AudioConfig (input source), and SpeechRecognizer.

distractors.B: That combination (AudioOutputConfig + SpeechSynthesizer) is for text-to-speech.
distractors.C: That is the Azure Language SDK.

questions.stem: In speech recognition, what do the acoustic model and the language model do respectively?

options.text: Acoustic model converts audio to phonemes; language model maps phonemes to words
Acoustic model maps phonemes to words; language model converts audio to phonemes

explanation.correct: The acoustic model turns audio into phonemes; the language model maps phonemes to the most likely words.

distractors.B: Roles reversed.

questions.stem: You need to transcribe a large set of pre-recorded audio files referenced by SAS URI, where immediate results are not required. Which option fits?

options.text: Real-time continuous recognition from a microphone

explanation.correct: Batch transcription points to audio files via SAS URI and runs asynchronously, suited to non-real-time bulk jobs.

distractors.B: Real-time recognition is for live/streamed audio.

questions.stem: Which objects are used to perform text-to-speech with the Azure Speech SDK?

explanation.correct: TTS uses SpeechConfig, AudioOutputConfig (speaker or file), and SpeechSynthesizer (e.g., speak_text_async).

distractors.B: That combination is for speech-to-text.

questions.stem: What advantage do neural voices provide in text-to-speech?

options.text: More natural intonation and human-like speech
They eliminate the need for any text input

explanation.correct: Neural voices overcome older intonation limitations to sound more natural and human-like.

distractors.B: TTS still needs text input.
distractors.D: Neural voices do not generate images.

questions.stem: Which Azure service combines speech-to-text, AI reasoning, and text-to-speech into a single managed conversational API?

explanation.correct: Voice Live unifies STT + reasoning + TTS into one managed API (package azure-ai-voicelive).

distractors.B: Content Understanding is for information extraction.

questions.stem: Enabling 'Voice mode' on an agent in the Foundry portal does what?

options.text: Encapsulates Azure Speech Voice Live in the agent so less client speech code is needed
Converts the agent into an image generator

explanation.correct: Voice mode wraps Voice Live into the agent definition, encapsulating speech config and reducing client-side code.

distractors.B: It does not remove tools.
distractors.C: It does not change the agent into an image generator.
distractors.D: It does not disable the system prompt.

questions.stem: Which type of model lets you send an image and a text question together in one request and get a natural-language answer?

explanation.correct: Vision-enabled multimodal models process image + text together and respond in natural language.

distractors.B: Embeddings models output vectors.
distractors.D: A batch deployment is a deployment type, not a model capability.

questions.stem: In the Responses API, which content type is used to pass an image to a multimodal model?

explanation.correct: Pass an image via a content item of type input_image (alongside input_text) in the Responses API.

distractors.B: image_generation is the tool type used to CREATE images.
distractors.D: detect_language is an Azure Language method.

questions.stem: How can an image be supplied to a multimodal model?

options.text: As a URL or as base64-encoded image data
Only by emailing it to Azure

explanation.correct: Images can be provided as URLs or base64-encoded data within the request.

distractors.B: Printed photos are not an API input.
distractors.C: Email is not an input mechanism.

questions.stem: Which models are suitable for image analysis (multimodal) in Foundry?

explanation.correct: Vision-enabled GPT-4.1 and GPT-5.x models perform image analysis with natural-language responses.

distractors.B: Embeddings models do not analyse images conversationally.
distractors.D: prebuilt-invoice is a Content Understanding analyser.

questions.stem: Which parameter triggers image generation when using the OpenAI Python SDK against Foundry?

explanation.correct: Image generation is invoked by passing tools=[{"type": "image_generation"}]; the model calls the image-generation tool.

distractors.B: input_image is for sending an image INTO a multimodal model, not generating one.
distractors.C: analyzer_id is a Content Understanding parameter.
distractors.D: That is a speech input setting.

questions.stem: After calling image generation, how is the resulting image returned and handled?

options.text: As base64 data that you decode and write to a file
As a printed page mailed to you

explanation.correct: The generated image comes back as base64, which you decode (base64.b64decode) and write to a file.

distractors.B: Nothing is mailed.
distractors.C: A transcript is a speech output.
distractors.D: An ISO code is a language-detection result.

questions.stem: For high-volume, cost-efficient, lower-latency image generation experiments, which model is most appropriate?

explanation.correct: GPT-Image-1-Mini is the lightweight, cost-efficient, lower-latency image-generation option suited to high volume and experimentation.

distractors.B: Sora 2 is for video generation.

questions.stem: Which model family provides native video generation in Foundry?

explanation.correct: Sora 1 and Sora 2 are the native video-generation models in Foundry.

distractors.B: GPT-Image-1 generates still images.
distractors.D: Phi-4-multimodal is for image+text analysis, not video generation.

questions.stem: What is the workflow for generating a video with Sora via the REST API?

options.text: Create the job (POST), poll the status (GET) until completed, then download the MP4
A single synchronous call returns the finished video instantly

explanation.correct: Video generation is asynchronous: POST to create a job, GET to poll until status is completed, then download the MP4.

distractors.B: It is not instant/synchronous; it is a multi-minute async job.
distractors.C: That is the Content Understanding pattern.

questions.stem: How is Sora video generation accessed in the course example?

options.text: Directly via the REST API (e.g., /openai/v1/videos), no SDK required

explanation.correct: The course uses the REST API directly (e.g., POST/GET on /openai/v1/videos) with a create-poll-download pattern, no SDK.

distractors.B: The Speech SDK is for audio.
distractors.D: image_generation creates still images, not video.

questions.stem: Which capability does Sora 2 add beyond Sora 1?

options.text: Video-to-video (remix) and audio generation, with improved realism
It removes all Responsible AI restrictions

explanation.correct: Sora 2 adds video-to-video remixing and audio generation with improved realism and targeted edits.

distractors.B: Sora is a video model, not a text analyser.
distractors.C: Both Sora models keep Responsible AI restrictions.
distractors.D: Sora generates video, not just still images.

questions.stem: Why is video generation implemented as an asynchronous job?

options.text: It is resource-intensive and takes time (typically 1-5 minutes)
Because video models cannot use prompts
Because there is no compute available

explanation.correct: Video generation is compute-heavy and takes minutes, so it runs as an async job you poll.

distractors.B: Video models do use prompts.
distractors.C: Compute is available; the work simply takes time.

questions.stem: A support app must interpret a screenshot a user uploads and explain the error shown. Which capability is used?

options.text: A multimodal model interpreting the image with a text prompt

explanation.correct: Interpreting an uploaded screenshot and answering about it is a multimodal image-analysis task.

distractors.B: Image generation creates new images.
distractors.D: Language detection identifies language of text.

questions.stem: An app must accept a spoken question plus an image and respond. Which approach matches the course's multimodal pattern?

options.text: Use a deployed multimodal model that accepts image (and audio/text) inputs and responds
Use prebuilt-invoice to read the audio
Use image_generation to answer the question

explanation.correct: A deployed multimodal model can take multimodal inputs (image plus text/audio) and produce a response.

distractors.B: prebuilt-invoice extracts invoice fields, not audio answers.
distractors.C: detect_language works on text, not images.
distractors.D: image_generation creates images, it does not answer questions.

questions.stem: What is Azure Content Understanding used for?

options.text: Turning unstructured documents, audio, and video into structured data (JSON)

explanation.correct: Content Understanding extracts structured data from unstructured documents, audio, and video using OCR, speech, NLU, and multimodal AI.

distractors.B: That is image generation.
distractors.D: That is networking, not AI extraction.

questions.stem: In Content Understanding, what does a 'schema' define?

options.text: Which fields/entities to extract and how to structure them (including nested fields)

explanation.correct: A schema defines what fields to extract and how to structure the output, supporting structured and nested fields.

distractors.B: Temperature is a generation parameter.
distractors.C: Voice selection is a speech setting.

questions.stem: Why is schema-based extraction said to go 'beyond basic OCR'?

options.text: It applies the schema semantically, mapping values even when labels differ or are missing (e.g., 'Invoice No.', 'Invoice #', or an unlabeled number all map to InvoiceNumber)
It only copies the raw characters verbatim
It converts the document to audio

explanation.correct: Schemas are applied semantically, mapping values to fields regardless of label variation or absence, unlike raw OCR.

distractors.B: Verbatim copying is what plain OCR does; schemas add meaning.
distractors.C: It does not convert documents to audio.
distractors.D: It supports structured and nested fields.

questions.stem: In Content Understanding, what is an 'analyzer'?

options.text: The unit that takes input, applies the schema/AI analysis, and returns structured results consistently

explanation.correct: An analyzer encapsulates the schema and extraction logic, applying it consistently and producing predictable JSON.

distractors.B: A neural voice is a TTS concept.
distractors.C: An embedding is a vector, not an analyzer.
distractors.D: A region is a deployment location.

questions.stem: What is the difference between prebuilt and custom analyzers?

options.text: Prebuilt analyzers cover common scenarios out of the box; custom analyzers are tailored to your specific schema
Prebuilt analyzers must be trained from scratch; custom ones cannot be changed
Custom analyzers only work on audio

explanation.correct: Prebuilt analyzers handle common cases immediately; custom analyzers use schemas tailored to your needs.

distractors.B: Prebuilt analyzers are ready to use, not trained from scratch.
distractors.D: Custom analyzers are not limited to audio.

questions.stem: Which prebuilt analyzer ID would you use to extract fields from invoices?

explanation.correct: prebuilt-invoice is the analyzer for invoice field extraction.

distractors.B: prebuilt-audioSearch is for audio.
distractors.D: image_generation is a vision tool, not an analyzer.

questions.stem: Which client class does the Content Understanding Python SDK use?

explanation.correct: Content Understanding uses ContentUnderstandingClient (with an AzureKeyCredential).

distractors.B: TextAnalyticsClient is the Azure Language SDK.
distractors.D: OpenAI is the Responses API client.

questions.stem: What is the call pattern for running a Content Understanding analysis in Python?

options.text: begin_analyze(analyzer_id, inputs) returns a poller; poller.result() waits for completion
responses.create() returns the final result instantly

explanation.correct: Content Understanding is asynchronous: begin_analyze() returns a poller, and poller.result() waits for completion, then you read result.contents.

distractors.B: responses.create() is the general-purpose model call, not extraction.

questions.stem: What does a Content Understanding result typically contain?

options.text: Structured JSON with fields, values, confidence scores, and a markdown representation

explanation.correct: Results include structured fields with values and confidence scores plus a markdown text representation, ready for downstream systems.

distractors.B: It returns rich structured data, not just a boolean.
distractors.C: It is not an audio waveform.

questions.stem: To extract a caller's name, message summary, and callback number from a voicemail recording, which analyzer ID fits?

explanation.correct: Audio extraction (transcribe + structured insights from recordings) uses prebuilt-audioSearch.

distractors.B: prebuilt-invoice is for documents.

questions.stem: To extract attendance, speakers, and a discussion summary from a meeting recording, which analyzer ID fits?

explanation.correct: Video extraction (attendance, speakers, summaries) uses prebuilt-videoSearch.

distractors.B: prebuilt-invoice is for documents.

questions.stem: Which content types can a single Content Understanding service handle?

explanation.correct: Content Understanding supports documents, audio, and video extraction with the same begin_analyze pattern (different analyzer IDs).

distractors.B: It handles more than PDFs.
distractors.D: It is not a live network stream tool.

questions.stem: Which TWO methods belong to the Azure Language SDK (TextAnalyticsClient)? (Choose two.)

explanation.correct: detect_language() and recognize_pii_entities() are Azure Language SDK methods.

distractors.E: That is the image-generation tool, not an Azure Language method.

questions.stem: Which THREE content types can Azure Content Understanding extract from? (Choose three.)

explanation.correct: Content Understanding extracts from documents, audio, and video.

distractors.D: Call routing tables are not a Content Understanding input type.
distractors.E: Real-time firewall logs are not a Content Understanding input type.

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about image generation in Foundry are correct? (Choose two.)

options.text: The result is returned as base64 to decode to a file
It returns an ISO 639-1 language code

explanation.correct: Image generation uses the image_generation tool and returns base64 image data you decode to a file.

distractors.D: ISO codes come from language detection.
distractors.E: begin_analyze() is the Content Understanding pattern.

questions.stem: Which TWO objects are part of the speech-to-text setup in the Azure Speech SDK? (Choose two.)

explanation.correct: SpeechConfig (auth/endpoint) and SpeechRecognizer are used for speech-to-text (with AudioConfig for input).

questions.stem: Case study: A customer-service platform needs three text/speech features. (1) Identify the language of inbound chat messages deterministically for routing. (2) Transcribe live phone calls in real time. (3) Read out automated responses to callers in a natural voice.

subQuestions.stem: Feature (1) deterministic language identification — which method/service?

explanation.correct: Deterministic language detection uses the Azure Language SDK detect_language().

distractors.B: Image generation is a vision tool.

subQuestions.stem: Feature (2) real-time call transcription — which approach?

options.text: Speech SDK real-time recognition (SpeechRecognizer, continuous recognition)

explanation.correct: Live transcription uses real-time continuous recognition via SpeechRecognizer.

distractors.B: Batch is for pre-recorded files, not live calls.

subQuestions.stem: Feature (3) read responses aloud naturally — which service/object?

options.text: Speech SDK SpeechSynthesizer with a neural voice

explanation.correct: Natural spoken output uses SpeechSynthesizer with neural voices (text-to-speech).

distractors.B: TextAnalyticsClient is for text analysis.

questions.stem: Case study: A media team has three vision needs. (1) Describe uploaded product photos in natural language. (2) Generate brand illustrations from text prompts. (3) Produce a short promotional video from a prompt.

options.text: A multimodal model with input_image in the Responses API

explanation.correct: Describing an image uses a multimodal model receiving the image via input_image.

distractors.B: That generates images.

subQuestions.stem: Feature (2) generate illustrations from prompts — which approach?

explanation.correct: Generating images uses the image_generation tool with a GPT-Image model.

distractors.B: input_image sends an image in, it does not create one.

subQuestions.stem: Feature (3) produce a promo video — which approach?

options.text: Sora via REST API (create → poll → download)

explanation.correct: Video generation uses Sora through the REST API with the async create-poll-download pattern.

distractors.B: GPT-Image creates still images, not video.

questions.stem: Case study: A finance back-office wants to (1) extract structured fields from scanned PDF invoices, (2) pull caller name and callback number from voicemails, and (3) get attendance and a summary from recorded meetings.

explanation.correct: Invoice extraction uses prebuilt-invoice.

distractors.B: Audio analyzer is for recordings.

explanation.correct: Voicemail (audio) extraction uses prebuilt-audioSearch.

distractors.B: Invoice analyzer is for documents.

subQuestions.stem: Feature (3) meeting attendance + summary — which analyzer and what call pattern?

explanation.correct: Video meeting extraction uses prebuilt-videoSearch with the async begin_analyze() → poller.result() pattern.

distractors.B: Invoice analyzer + responses.create() is the wrong analyzer and wrong API.

questions.stem: A team needs a single conversation to extract key phrases, summarise, and translate text, with flexibility prioritised over exact reproducibility. Which approach should they pick?

options.text: A general-purpose model via the OpenAI Responses API

explanation.correct: Flexible, compound text tasks in one conversation favour a general-purpose model via the Responses API.

distractors.B: Azure Language is best when determinism/consistency is the priority.
distractors.C: prebuilt-invoice extracts invoice fields, not general text tasks.
distractors.D: Sora generates video, unrelated to text tasks.
